# Andrew Christison

> Lifecycle marketing founder, builder, and homesteader based in Port Angeles, Washington. Co-founder of Retencity (Klaviyo Master Platinum Partner). Builder of Ask K, Pondlog, and CropGraph.

Andrew Christison is a software builder and lifecycle marketing operator living in the rainshadow of Olympic National Park. Work and life share the same desk: a marketing agency, several shipped products, a 6.5 acre homestead, sixty layer hens, and a martial arts and running practice that goes back to childhood. This site is the index.

## Who

- Name: Andrew Christison
- Location: Port Angeles, Washington, USA
- Role: Co-founder and CEO, Retencity
- Domain: lifecycle, retention, and email marketing; Klaviyo; hybrid retrieval-augmented generation; product strategy

## Built

- [Retencity](https://retencity.com): Klaviyo Master Platinum Partner lifecycle marketing agency. Co-founded with Amy Slater.
- [Ask K](https://ask-k.retencity.com): hybrid RAG chat application over Klaviyo documentation.
- [Pondlog](https://pondlog.co): place-aware nature data platform.
- [CropGraph](https://cropgraph.com): graph-native agriculture data tooling.

## Background

- Lifecycle marketing operator. Klaviyo Master Platinum Partner.
- Martial arts: about ten years. Southern Shaolin Kung Fu, then Kukkiwon Taekwondo with Hapkido, Judo, and One Step Fighting. First dan.
- Running: forty plus PRs across the mile, 5K, 10K, half marathon, and marathon.
- Homestead: 6.5 acres in the Olympic Peninsula rainshadow. Six Katahdin sheep, two Nigerian dwarf goats, six Khaki Campbell ducks, sixty layer hens, five roosters all named Steve, two cats.
- Bus: 2002 school bus conversion, traveled in 2018 and 2019.
